rulings.Calendar Matter: CASE STATUS HEARING FOR ORDER ADDRESSING CERTAIN CASE ADMINISTRATION ISSUES. ALL FURTHER BRIEFINGS DUE BY DECEMBER 6, 2024. EX PARTE APLICATION TO SERVE AS DEFENDANT HUDSON ADVISORS, L.P.'S MOVING PAPERS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 9, 2025, line 1, CASE STATUS HEARING FOR ORDER ADDRESSING CERTAIN CASE ADMINISTRATION ISSUES. ALL FURTHER BRIEFINGS DUE BY DECEMBER 6, 2024. EX PARTE APLICATION TO SERVE AS DEFENDANT HUDSON ADVISORS, L.P.'S MOVING PAPERS Off calendar. The court dismissed the action on July 28, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHANG H. AHN ET AL VS. BUCHALTER, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Order To Appear At Creditor'S Examination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 9, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANT HR E&I, INC.'S Motion To Quash Order To Appear At Creditor'S Examination Judgment debtor HR E&I moves to quash an order to appear at a creditor's examination. The motion is denied as moot; the opposing papers show that the date for the examination has passed and counsel for judgment creditor did not appear. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address = (302/CVA)
